name: Remote Receiver
description: The `remote_receiver` binary sensor lets you track when a button on a remote control is pressed.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the binary sensor. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the binary sensor in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the sensor. See [https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/binary_sensor/#device-class](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/binary_sensor/#device-class) for a list of available options.

config_entries.label: Receiver ID
config_entries.description: The remote receiver to receive the remote code with. Required if multiple receivers configured.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Abbwelcome
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded ABB-Welcome code with the given data, see the [transmitter description](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-transmit_abbwelcome) for more info.

config_entries.label: Destination Address
config_entries.description: The destination address to trigger on.

config_entries.label: Message Type
config_entries.description: The message type to trigger on.

config_entries.label: Source Address
config_entries.description: The source address to trigger on.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The code to listen for. Usually you only need to copy this directly from the dumper output. Defaults to `[]`

config_entries.label: Message ID
config_entries.description: The random message ID to trigger on, see dumper output for more info. Defaults to any ID.

config_entries.label: Retransmission
config_entries.description: `true` if the message was re-transmitted. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Three Byte Address
config_entries.description: The length of the source and destination address. `false` means two bytes and `true` means three bytes. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Aeha
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded AEHA remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The address to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The code to listen for, see [transmitter description](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-transmit_aeha) for more info. Usually you only need to copy this directly from the dumper output.

config_entries.label: Beo4
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded B&O Beo4 infrared remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The 8-bit command to listen for, e.g. 0x00=number0, 0x0C=standby,..., see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Source
config_entries.description: The 8-bit source to trigger on, e.g. 0x00=video, 0x01=audio,..., see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Brennenstuhl
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded brennenstuhl RF code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Code
config_entries.description: The 24-bit code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Byronsx
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Byron SX Doorbell RF remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The 8-bit ID code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The 4-bit command to listen for. If omitted, will match on any command.

config_entries.label: Canalsat
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded CanalSat remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The address (or subdevice) to trigger on, see dumper output for more info. Defaults to `0`.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The command to listen for.

config_entries.label: Device
config_entries.description: The device to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Canalsatld
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded CanalSatLD remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The address (or subdevice) to trigger on, see dumper output for more info. Defaults to `0`.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The command to listen for.

config_entries.label: Device
config_entries.description: The device to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Coolix
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Coolix remote code with the given data. It is possible to directly specify a 24-bit code, it will be checked for a match to at least one of the two received packets. The main configuration scheme is below.

config_entries.label: First
config_entries.description: The first 24-bit Coolix code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Second
config_entries.description: The second 24-bit Coolix code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info. If not set, trigger on only single non-strict packet, specified by the `first` parameter.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Dish
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Dish Network remote code with the given data. Beware that Dish remotes use a different carrier frequency (57.6kHz) that many receiver hardware don't decode.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The number of the receiver to target, between 1 and 16 inclusive. Defaults to `1`.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The Dish command to listen for, between 0 and 63 inclusive.

config_entries.label: Dooya
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Dooya RF remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: The 24-bit ID code to trigger on.

config_entries.label: Button
config_entries.description: The 4-bit button to listen for.

config_entries.label: Channel
config_entries.description: The 8-bit channel to listen for.

config_entries.label: Check
config_entries.description: The 4-bit check to listen for. Includes an indication that a button is being held down.

config_entries.label: Drayton
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Drayton Digistat RF remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The 16-bit ID code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Channel
config_entries.description: The 7-bit switch/channel to listen for.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The 5-bit command to listen for.

config_entries.label: Dyson
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded dyson cool AM07 infrared remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Code
config_entries.description: The 16-bit code to trigger on, e.g. 0x1200=power, 0x1215=fan++,0x122a=swing..., see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Index
config_entries.description: The 8-bit rolling index [0..3], to be increased with every transmit, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Filters
config_entries.description: A list of filters to apply on the binary sensor values such as inverting signals. See [Binary Sensor Filters](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor#binary_sensor-filters).

config_entries.label: Gobox
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Go-Box remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Code
config_entries.description: The Go-Box code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Haier
config_entries.description: Trigger on a Haier remote code with the given code.

config_entries.label: Code
config_entries.description: The code to listen for, see [transmitter description](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-transmit_haier) for more info. Usually you only need to copy this directly from the dumper output.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Jvc
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded JVC remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The JVC code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Keeloq
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded KeeLoq RF remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The 32-bit ID code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The 8-bit switch/command to listen for. If omitted, will match on any command/button.

config_entries.label: Lg
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded LG remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The LG code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Nbits
config_entries.description: The number of bits of the remote code. Defaults to `28`.

config_entries.label: Magiquest
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded MagiQuest wand remote code with the given wand ID.

config_entries.label: Wand ID
config_entries.description: The MagiQuest wand ID to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Magnitude
config_entries.description: The magnitude of swishes and swirls of the wand. If omitted, will match on any activation of the wand.

config_entries.label: Midea
config_entries.description: Trigger on a Midea remote code with the given code.

config_entries.label: Code
config_entries.description: The code to listen for, see [transmitter description](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-transmit_midea) for more info. Usually you only need to copy first 5 bytes directly from the dumper output.

config_entries.label: Mirage
config_entries.description: Trigger on a Mirage remote code with the given code.

config_entries.label: Code
config_entries.description: The code to listen for, see [transmitter description](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-transmit_mirage) for more info. Usually you only need to copy this directly from the dumper output.

config_entries.label: Nec
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded NEC remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The address to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The NEC command to listen for.

config_entries.label: Nexa
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Nexa RF code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Level
config_entries.description: The Nexa level code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Channel
config_entries.description: The Nexa channel code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Device
config_entries.description: The Nexa device code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Group
config_entries.description: The Nexa group code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: State
config_entries.description: The Nexa state code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Panasonic
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Panasonic remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The address to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The command.

config_entries.label: Pioneer
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Pioneer remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Rc Code 1
config_entries.description: The remote control code to trigger on, see dumper output for more details.

config_entries.label: Pronto
config_entries.description: Trigger on a Pronto remote code with the given code.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The code to listen for, see [transmitter description](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-transmit_pronto) for more info. Usually you only need to copy this directly from the dumper output.

config_entries.label: Delta
config_entries.description: This parameter allows you to manually specify the allowed difference between what Pronto code is specified, and what IR signal has been sent by the remote control.

config_entries.label: Raw
config_entries.description: Trigger on a raw remote code with the given code.

config_entries.label: Code
config_entries.description: The code to listen for, see [transmitter description](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-transmit_raw) for more info. Usually you only need to copy this directly from the dumper output.

config_entries.label: Rc5
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded RC5 remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The address to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The RC5 command to listen for.

config_entries.label: Rc6
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded RC6 remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The address to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The RC6 command to listen for.

config_entries.label: Rc Switch Raw
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded RC Switch raw remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Code
config_entries.description: The remote code to listen for, copy this from the dumper output. To ignore a bit in the received data, use `x` at that place in the **code**.

config_entries.label: Protocol
config_entries.description: The RC Switch protocol to use, see [RC Switch Protocol](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-rc_switch-protocol) for more info.

config_entries.label: Rc Switch Type A
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded RC Switch Type A remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Device
config_entries.description: The device in the group, binary string.

config_entries.label: Group
config_entries.description: The group, binary string.

config_entries.label: State
config_entries.description: The on/off state to trigger on.

config_entries.label: Protocol
config_entries.description: The RC Switch protocol to use, see [RC Switch Protocol](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-rc_switch-protocol) for more info.

config_entries.label: Rc Switch Type B
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded RC Switch Type B remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The address, int from 1 to 4.

config_entries.label: Channel
config_entries.description: The channel, int from 1 to 4.

config_entries.label: State
config_entries.description: The on/off state to trigger on.

config_entries.label: Protocol
config_entries.description: The RC Switch protocol to use, see [RC Switch Protocol](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-rc_switch-protocol) for more info.

config_entries.label: Rc Switch Type C
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded RC Switch Type C remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Device
config_entries.description: The device. Range is 1 to 4.

config_entries.label: Family
config_entries.description: The family. Range is `a` to `p`.

config_entries.label: Group
config_entries.description: The group. Range is 1 to 4.

config_entries.label: State
config_entries.description: The on/off state to trigger on.

config_entries.label: Protocol
config_entries.description: The RC Switch protocol to use, see [RC Switch Protocol](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-rc_switch-protocol) for more info.

config_entries.label: Rc Switch Type D
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded RC Switch Type D remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Device
config_entries.description: The device. Range is 1 to 3.

config_entries.label: Group
config_entries.description: The group. Range is 1 to 4.

config_entries.label: State
config_entries.description: The on/off state to trigger on.

config_entries.label: Protocol
config_entries.description: The RC Switch protocol to use, see [RC Switch Protocol](https://esphome.io/components/remote_transmitter#remote_transmitter-rc_switch-protocol) for more info.

config_entries.label: Roomba
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Roomba remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The Roomba code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Samsung
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Samsung remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The data to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Nbits
config_entries.description: The number of bits of the remote code. Defaults to `32`.

config_entries.label: Samsung36
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Samsung36 remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The address to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The command.

config_entries.label: Sony
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Sony remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The Sony code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Nbits
config_entries.description: The number of bits of the remote code. Defaults to `12`.

config_entries.label: Symphony
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Symphony remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Data
config_entries.description: The Symphony code to trigger on, see dumper output for more info.

config_entries.label: Nbits
config_entries.description: The number of bits of the remote code. Typical values: `8`, `12`, or `16`.

config_entries.label: Toshiba AC
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Toshiba AC remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Rc Code 1
config_entries.description: The remote control code to trigger on, see dumper output for more details.

config_entries.label: Rc Code 2
config_entries.description: The second part of the remote control code to trigger on, see dumper output for more details.

config_entries.label: Toto
config_entries.description: Trigger on a decoded Toto remote code with the given data.

config_entries.label: Command
config_entries.description: The 1-byte Toto command code to trigger on. Range is 0 to 0xFF.

config_entries.label: Rc Code 1
config_entries.description: The first 4-bit Toto code (usually a command parameter) to trigger on. Range is 0 to 0xF.

config_entries.label: Rc Code 2
config_entries.description: The second 4-bit Toto code (usually a command parameter) to trigger on. Range is 0 to 0xF.

config_entries.label: Trigger On Initial State
config_entries.description: If true, any applicable triggers will be fired when the binary sensor state changes from `unknown` to a valid state. This applies to the first valid state set, and any valid state set after a `binary_sensor.invalidate_state` action has been executed. The default is `false`.
